CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Validate Webhook Signatures and Origin on All Incoming Callbacks
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Network Security**. Verifies that every AI agent validates the cryptographic signature and origin of all incoming webhook callbacks before processing them. Agents that accept webhooks without signature verification can be triggered by forged or replayed payloads, allowing attackers to manipulate agent behaviour without valid credentials.
Risk: An agent that accepts unauthenticated webhooks can be triggered by any actor who discovers the webhook URL. Attackers can forge event payloads to trigger agent actions (e.g., code execution, data retrieval), replay captured legitimate events, or inject malicious content into the agent's context.

Code.Other:
1. Set validates_webhooks: true in the agent manifest.
2. Verify the HMAC-SHA256 signature provided in the webhook header (e.g., X-Hub-Signature-256) before processing any payload.
3. Include a timestamp in the signed payload and reject requests where the timestamp is more than 5 minutes old to prevent replay attacks.
4. Allowlist webhook source IP ranges where the provider publishes them.

Recommendation.Text: Validate the HMAC signature and timestamp on every incoming webhook before processing. Reject payloads with invalid signatures, missing signatures, or stale timestamps. Rotate webhook secrets regularly.

Notes: ASPM-046 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
